claims.summary: 7 RCTs (n=491). Mean HAM-A drop 41% vs 24% placebo. Effect robust across doses 250–600 mg/d.

clinical_protocol.standard: The 7 RCTs cited used standardized extracts (KSM-66, Sensoril) containing 21–30mg of withanolides daily, divided into 2 doses.
clinical_protocol.traditional_equivalence: This typically requires 3–6g of high-quality whole root powder prepared as a decoction (simmered in milk or fat) to achieve comparable withanolide levels.

provenance.commercial_drift_note: Modern clinical trials use standardized extracts (KSM-66, Sensoril) containing 21–30mg withanolides daily. Traditional use involves 3–6g whole root powder prepared as a decoction in milk or fat. Marketing often conflates extract study results with generic root powder products that have much lower withanolide content.

claims.summary: 3 RCTs show modest PSQI improvements. Effect size smaller than valerian; mostly overlaps anxiolytic benefit.

clinical_protocol.standard: The 3 RCTs used standardized extracts (KSM-66, Sensoril) at 250–600mg daily, typically taken before bed.
clinical_protocol.traditional_equivalence: Traditional sleep use involves 3–5g root powder in warm milk or ghee, taken 30–60 minutes before bedtime.

provenance.commercial_drift_note: Modern clinical trials use standardized extracts (KSM-66, Sensoril) at 250–600mg daily. Traditional sleep use involves 3–5g root powder in warm milk or ghee. Marketing often conflates extract study results with generic root powder products that have much lower withanolide content.

claims.summary: One open-label 46-subject trial reported sperm count +167%; never replicated in an RCT.

provenance.commercial_drift_note: The single open-label trial used standardized extract (225mg withanolides). Traditional fertility tonics use whole root powder in larger doses (3–5g) with milk/ghee. Marketing often cites this study for generic root powder products without noting the extract-specific dose.

claims.summary: Small trials suggest mild TSH-lowering; raises T3/T4 in subclinical hypothyroid. Monitor on thyroid meds.

provenance.commercial_drift_note: The single RCT used standardized extract (600mg withanolides). Traditional thyroid support uses whole root powder in larger doses (3–5g) with milk/ghee. Marketing often cites this study for generic root powder products without noting the extract-specific dose.

claims.summary: No modern clinical trials. Classical rejuvenative use in Ayurveda for > 2,000 years.
